frameworks.name: latent structure learning
frameworks.one_line: Inferring unobserved social group categories beyond explicit labels

frameworks.name: dyadic similarity
frameworks.one_line: Pairwise interpersonal similarity as alternative input to allyship

frameworks.name: model-based fMRI
frameworks.one_line: Trial-by-trial computational regressors mapped onto BOLD signal

core_question: How does the brain learn and represent latent social group boundaries beyond explicit labels and dyadic similarity?
key_assumptions: Allyship judgments can be derived from a computational latent-structure model
Dyadic similarity and latent group structure are dissociable inputs to social representations
Trial-by-trial model estimates index neural computations
manipulations: Compared dyadic-similarity-based vs latent-structure-based allyship estimates
Trial-by-trial variation in model-derived allyship signals
key_findings: Dyadic similarity allyship estimates correlated with medial prefrontal cortex / pregenual anterior cingulate
Latent group structure allyship estimates correlated with right anterior insula
Right anterior insula variability improved prediction of ally-choice behavior
Pregenual anterior cingulate variability did not improve choice prediction
interpretations: Separate brain areas encode dyadic similarity vs latent social group structure
Right anterior insula carries behaviorally relevant latent social-structure signals
